hypothesis: Removing the source7 special-case bypass and using the normal DFII byte/phase path with slot7->slot8 remap should keep source7 observable if the issue was bypass-only routing.

source_change.description: V110 removes the dfii_phasecmd_index==7 direct-lane7 write bypass in DFII-byte-path and restores the normal dfii_byte_phase_assoc_word5 routing with source7 mapped onto slot8 through remap logic.

source7_summary.interpretation: Source7 remains not fixed and maps only into a single-bit pattern that does not match expected tag occupancy.

source8_summary.interpretation: Source8 still appears alive but weakly aligned in the same low-lane slot context as source7, not a clean direct remap path for phase association.

decision.conclusion: Removing the DFII phasecmd==7 bypass did not restore correct source7 behavior; this does not remove the alias-collapse path. The issue is deeper than the old special-case gate.
decision.next_gate: Keep source7 path unchanged and proceed with native-write path proofing: verify DFI write enqueue and WDF FIFO occupancy/push before changing further lane associations.
